file_insights.changes: Added clarity to User model managers by documenting the purpose of two manager instances: 'objects' (default manager with create_user/create_superuser methods) and 'all_objects' (includes ALL records including soft-deleted ones)
file_insights.impact: Documentation improvement for better code maintainability and clarity about manager behavior

file_insights.changes: Fixed incorrect reference to all_objects manager in soft delete test. Changed from 'User.objects.model.all_objects.filter()' to 'User.all_objects.filter()'
file_insights.impact: Bug fix - corrected test to use proper manager access pattern, improves test correctness

patterns_discovered.description: User model implements dual-manager pattern for soft deletes: default 'objects' manager filters out deleted records, while 'all_objects' manager provides access to all records including soft-deleted ones
patterns_discovered.significance: Enables graceful handling of soft deletes with transparent filtering by default while preserving recovery capabilities

patterns_discovered.description: Bug discovered during test execution led to fixing both test code and improving model documentation
patterns_discovered.significance: Demonstrates how test failures can surface code clarity issues beyond just logic bugs

gotchas_discovered.issue: Incorrect manager access pattern in test
gotchas_discovered.description: Original test used 'User.objects.model.all_objects.filter()' which is overly verbose and incorrect. Correct pattern is 'User.all_objects.filter()'
gotchas_discovered.lesson: Direct manager access on model is cleaner than accessing through model property on another manager

gotchas_discovered.description: The purpose of having two separate managers wasn't clearly documented in the code, which could confuse developers about proper usage
gotchas_discovered.lesson: Dual-manager patterns for soft deletes require explicit documentation of which manager to use in which scenarios

approach_outcome.summary: Successfully completed test suite execution with bug fixes for soft delete manager access pattern
key_achievements: Fixed incorrect manager access pattern in test (objects.model.all_objects → all_objects)
Enhanced code documentation for User model managers
approach_outcome.recovery_notes: First two attempts failed with pending status, third attempt successfully executed the test suite and identified/fixed manager access issues

recommendations.recommendation: Establish guidelines for soft-delete patterns that clearly document when to use filtered vs unfiltered managers
recommendations.rationale: Prevents future confusion about dual-manager implementations and ensures consistent usage across the codebase

recommendations.recommendation: Add integration tests that explicitly verify manager filtering behavior (deleted vs non-deleted records)
recommendations.rationale: Ensures soft-delete logic is functioning as expected and prevents regression of manager access patterns

recommendations.recommendation: Add inline comments explaining the all_objects manager purpose and typical use cases
recommendations.rationale: Improves code comprehensibility for new developers and future maintainers
